title: melica subulata var. pammelii
intro: Melica subulata var. pammelii, also known as Pammel's melic grass, is a type of perennial grass native to North America, specifically in the central and western regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Melica subulata var. pammelii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
